description: Address review findings on auto-review-after-loop: (1) whitespace guard in ProcessLauncher's md path, (2) rustdoc clarifications for prd_md_path trust boundary + maybe_fire TOCTOU, (3) zero-threshold regression test for should_fire, (4) hoist chain_base snapshot out of make_result closure in batch.rs.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <scope>` (full suite at REVIEW-001 only)
No `unwrap()` in production paths — use `map_err` / `?` / explicit `match`
No breaking changes to existing public APIs (auto-review-after-loop's API is shipped; these are additive guards + docs + tests + a closure hoist)
Auto-review never causes the loop to exit non-zero — preflight failures print a hint and return
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Auto-review must never alter the loop's exit code — preflight failures print a hint and return Ok
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Auto-review launch that masks a successful loop with its own failure — review launch errors must NEVER turn a passing loop into a failing one

Behavior changes to existing acceptance criteria of FEAT-001..006 / REVIEW-001 / REFACTOR-001 from the original auto-review-after-loop PRD (this PRD is purely additive review-fix work on the same branch)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: in `maybe_fire`, after the markdown path is resolved, a whitespace check suppresses launch when `md.to_string_lossy().chars().any(char::is_whitespace)` is true.
Positive: suppression prints a `[auto-review]` hint to stderr naming the path and recommending rename / manual /review-loop.

Positive: regression test `maybe_fire_suppresses_when_md_path_contains_whitespace` uses `CapturingLauncher`; asserts `launcher.calls` is empty after `maybe_fire` returns; uses temp files with space-containing names so `prd_md_path` resolves.
Positive: existing space-free path tests still pass (regression: launcher IS called when other gates are clean).
Negative: guard must NOT alter the loop's exit code — `maybe_fire`'s return type is `()`; this task does not change that.
Negative: guard must NOT use `unwrap()` or panic — `to_string_lossy()` is the right primitive (handles non-UTF-8 OS paths).
Negative: guard must NOT short-circuit BEFORE the existing TTY / missing-worktree / missing-md checks; placement is AFTER `prd_md_path` resolves.
Unit test: path containing a single ASCII space suppresses launch (CapturingLauncher.calls empty).
Unit test: path containing a tab character (`\t`) suppresses launch.
Unit test: clean space-free path proceeds to `launcher.launch` (calls.len() == 1) when other gates pass.
Known-bad: a guard that checks ONLY for `' '` (literal ASCII space) passes the single-space test but fails the tab/newline test — the `char::is_whitespace` approach must catch all Unicode whitespace.

Known-bad: a guard placed BEFORE `prd_md_path` would check `prd_json` (the .json input path) instead of the resolved `md` (the .md launch arg) — those can diverge if the user names the JSON without spaces but a sibling .md has spaces. The test exercises this by giving the temp .json and .md the same space-containing stem.

Failure mode: if the path is non-UTF-8 (OSError edge case on Linux), `to_string_lossy()` substitutes replacement chars and the guard correctly suppresses (rather than panicking on `.to_str().unwrap()`).
userStories.description:
Claude's slash-command parser re-tokenizes the body on whitespace, so passing `format!("/review-loop {}", md.display())` with a path containing spaces (e.g., `tasks/My PRD.md`) silently fragments into multiple tokens. Defense: in `maybe_fire`, after `prd_md_path` resolves the markdown path and BEFORE `launcher.launch(...)`, check whether the resolved path contains any whitespace char. If yes, print a recovery hint to stderr and return (same suppression pattern as the existing TTY / missing-md / missing-worktree gates).

DO: insert the guard immediately after the `let md = match prd_md_path(prd_json) { ... };` block at ~line 248. Use `md.to_string_lossy().chars().any(char::is_whitespace)`. Hint message must name the path and tell the user to rename or run /review-loop manually. Add a unit test using `CapturingLauncher` that asserts `launcher.calls.lock().unwrap().len() == 0` when the resolved md path contains a space.

DO NOT: quote the path inside the slash-command body (Claude's parser may or may not honor quotes; behavior is unverified — suppression is the safe default). Do NOT change the gate ordering of the existing checks. Do NOT add a `should_fire` parameter for whitespace — this is a launch-time boundary concern, not a decision-time concern.

Edge cases:
- Path with single space (`tasks/My PRD.md`): guard fires, launcher not called, exit code unchanged.
- Path with tab or newline: same — `char::is_whitespace` catches all Unicode whitespace.
- Path with no whitespace: launcher fires normally (regression).

Known-bad to guard against:
- Stripping only ASCII space (` `) misses tab/newline cases.
- Placing the guard before `prd_md_path` resolution: the input could be `tasks/foo.json` (space-free) but resolve to `tasks/foo.md` which is space-free anyway. Place AFTER resolution so the check covers the actual launch arg.
edgeCases: Path with single ASCII space: suppress, hint printed.
Path with tab `\t` or newline `\n`: suppress (Unicode whitespace).
Space-free path: proceed to launcher.launch (regression preserved).
Non-UTF-8 path: `to_string_lossy()` substitutes — guard still suppresses; never panic.

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test catches this — the `.md` path is the one Claude parses; the `.json` input is irrelevant to the bug.

failureModes.cause: Guard uses `==' '` or only ASCII space check

userStories.notes: File: src/loop_engine/auto_review.rs (~line 248). Existing suppression-gate pattern lives in the same function (~lines 207-247) — match its style: eprintln! with a `[auto-review]` prefix and an explicit recovery hint. CapturingLauncher harness is in the test module (~line 365 onward); follow `maybe_fire_*` test conventions. Learning [2419]: Slash-command argv re-tokenizes on whitespace inside the body — this task is the defensive fix for that footgun. Learning [2394]: maybe_fire is the shared gateway used by both main.rs and batch.rs, so this guard automatically applies to both call sites.
qualityDimensions: Guard placement after `prd_md_path` resolution — checks the actual launch argument, not the input
Uses `char::is_whitespace` to catch all Unicode whitespace (space, tab, newline, etc.), not just ASCII space
Uses `to_string_lossy()` so non-UTF-8 OS paths suppress safely instead of panicking
Hint message names the offending path and recommends an actionable recovery (rename or run manually)
Test uses the public `CapturingLauncher` harness with `calls.lock().unwrap().len()` assertion — the same shape as existing maybe_fire tests
userStories.title: Whitespace guard for PRD markdown path in maybe_fire

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: rustdoc above `prd_md_path` (~line 91-97) gains a `**Trust boundary**:` paragraph naming `prd_json` as operator-controlled and noting no path-escape detection.

Positive: above the `Some(wt) if !wt.exists() =>` arm at ~line 226 a `// Best-effort existence check...` comment names the TOCTOU race against `git worktree prune` and notes the launcher's own error path surfaces it.
Positive: new test `should_fire_zero_threshold_fires_when_other_gates_pass` lives in the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block immediately after `should_fire_boundary_equal_to_min`.
Positive: test asserts `should_fire(&enabled_decision(0), 0, false, 0)` is true and `should_fire(&enabled_decision(0), 0, false, 1)` is true.
Positive: test asserts `should_fire(&enabled_decision(0), 1, false, 5)` is false (exit_code gate) and `should_fire(&enabled_decision(0), 0, true, 5)` is false (was_stopped gate).
Negative: production code (`prd_md_path`, `maybe_fire`, `should_fire` bodies) is byte-identical except for the new doc comment / inline comment — no logic edits.
Negative: rustdoc additions are real triple-slash `///` doc-comments (not `//` line comments) above the function so `cargo doc` includes them.
Negative: must NOT remove or weaken existing tests `should_fire_blocked_by_threshold` / `should_fire_boundary_equal_to_min`.

Known-bad: a `should_fire` whose threshold check uses `>` instead of `>=` passes the existing `=== min` boundary test (3 >= 3 and 3 > 2 both true at min=3) but fails the new zero-threshold test (0 > 0 is false). The new test is the discriminator.

Failure mode: if `cargo doc` warnings fire on the new rustdoc paragraph (malformed markdown, broken intra-doc link), fix the formatting — `cargo doc 2>&1 | grep -i 'warning.*prd_md_path'` must be empty after this task.
userStories.description:
Two non-obvious assumptions in `auto_review.rs` deserve rustdoc notes so future maintainers don't re-discover them. Plus the `min_tasks=0` config-only-reachable code path lacks an explicit test.

DO: (1) append a `Trust boundary` paragraph to the rustdoc above `pub fn prd_md_path` noting that `prd_json` is operator-controlled (CLI argument) and no path-escape detection is performed. (2) add an inline comment above the `Some(wt) if !wt.exists() =>` arm in `maybe_fire` (~line 226) noting that the check is best-effort and a concurrent `git worktree prune` could race — the launcher's own error path handles the racy outcome. (3) add `should_fire_zero_threshold_fires_when_other_gates_pass` to the test module — asserts `min_tasks=0` fires when `tasks_completed` is 0 or 1, AND that other gates (exit_code, was_stopped) still block independently.

DO NOT: change any production code logic. This task is rustdoc-only on the production side plus one new test.

Edge cases:
- `enabled_decision(0)` with tasks_completed=0: should fire (other gates clean).
- `enabled_decision(0)` with tasks_completed=1: should fire.
- `enabled_decision(0)` with exit_code=1: should NOT fire (other gates block).
- `enabled_decision(0)` with was_stopped=true: should NOT fire.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Changing `>=` to `>` in `should_fire` would pass `min=3, tasks=3` (existing boundary test) but break `min=0, tasks=0` — the new test is the discriminator.
- Adding the test in the wrong module (outside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`) would fail to compile.
edgeCases: min_tasks=0, exit_code=1: blocked by exit_code gate.
min_tasks=0, was_stopped=true: blocked by stopped gate.

failureModes.cause: Test placed outside the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compilation fails because `enabled_decision`/`disabled_decision` helpers are test-local.

userStories.notes: File: src/loop_engine/auto_review.rs. Rustdoc location 1: above `pub fn prd_md_path` at ~line 98. Inline comment location: above the `Some(wt) if !wt.exists() =>` match arm at ~line 226. Test location: after `should_fire_boundary_equal_to_min` (~line 361) — use the existing `enabled_decision(0)` helper. No new imports needed. Learning [2419] is the upstream context for the rustdoc trust-boundary note (operator-controlled, no escape).
qualityDimensions: Rustdoc additions use `///` triple-slash form (cargo doc visible), not `//`
Inline TOCTOU comment names the specific racy operation (`git worktree prune`), not just 'race condition'
New test is structurally identical to its siblings — same helpers, same shape, no novel scaffolding
Production logic byte-identical except for comment additions (verifiable via `git diff --stat`)
userStories.title: Rustdoc clarifications + zero-threshold should_fire regression test

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: a single `let chain_base_snapshot = if chain { chain_base.clone() } else { None };` binding exists between `let exit_code = loop_result.exit_code;` (~line 648) and the `make_result` closure (~line 653).
Positive: `LoopRunConfig.chain_base` (~line 643) is rewritten to use `chain_base_snapshot.clone()` (or moved below the snapshot binding and use the same).
Positive: the `make_result` closure body uses `chain_base_snapshot.clone()` instead of `if chain { chain_base.clone() } else { None }`.
Positive: `grep -n 'chain_base.clone()' src/loop_engine/batch.rs` in the lines 630-720 range returns at most 1 hit (the snapshot binding RHS) — both prior duplicates collapse.
Positive: existing tests `test_prd_run_result_*`, `pick_review_target_*`, and `test_cleanup_*` pass unchanged (regression).
Negative: scope of `chain_base_snapshot` must be the per-PRD `for` iteration body — not lifted above the `for` loop.
Negative: the loop's `chain_base = result_branch_name;` reassignment at ~line 705 is UNCHANGED — only the closure-internal duplication is hoisted.
Negative: `make_result` closure must continue to capture by reference (the snapshot is a `let` binding above; `.clone()` inside the closure prevents move-out).
Unit test: existing `pick_review_target_*` tests still pass.

Known-bad: a refactor that unconditionally clones `chain_base` (drops the `if chain` guard) would set `LoopRunConfig.chain_base` to `Some(...)` when `chain` is false — `test_prd_run_result_*` should fail in that case (and if it doesn't, it's because no test exercises the chain=false path; add one).

Failure mode: if `cargo check` complains about a moved-out value in the closure, switch the snapshot type to `Option<&str>` borrowed for the closure or keep `.clone()` calls at each site (the latter is the design here).
userStories.description:
In the per-PRD loop body in `run_batch` (src/loop_engine/batch.rs, ~lines 633-707), the expression `if chain { chain_base.clone() } else { None }` appears twice: once when constructing `LoopRunConfig.chain_base` (~line 643) and once inside the `make_result` closure (~line 659). The closure re-evaluates this on every call (called at ~lines 668 and 681). Hoist it to a single `let chain_base_snapshot = if chain { chain_base.clone() } else { None };` BEFORE the closure definition, then reference `chain_base_snapshot.clone()` in both places.

DO: introduce a single binding `chain_base_snapshot` between `let exit_code = loop_result.exit_code;` and the closure definition, and use it in both `LoopRunConfig.chain_base` and the closure body. Reads must NOT happen after the loop's `chain_base = result_branch_name` reassignment at ~line 705 (the snapshot must capture pre-reassignment value).

DO NOT: change any observable behavior. This is a pure refactor — closure captures identical value, struct field gets identical value, downstream `PrdRunResult.chain_base` and `LoopRunConfig.chain_base` semantics unchanged. Do NOT widen the binding's scope outside the per-PRD `for` iteration.

Edge cases:
- `chain=false`: snapshot is None; both consumers receive None (unchanged).
- `chain=true` on first iteration: snapshot is None (chain_base starts None); both consumers receive None.
- `chain=true` on iteration N>0: snapshot is `Some(prev_branch_name)` captured before reassignment; both consumers receive that value (unchanged).

Known-bad to guard against:
- Hoisting AFTER the `chain_base = result_branch_name` reassignment at line 705 would snapshot the WRONG value for the next iteration's closure call (but the closure is only invoked within the same iteration before line 705 — still, placement before the closure DEFINITION is the correct invariant).
- Removing the `if chain` guard and unconditionally cloning would change `chain_base_snapshot` from `None` to `Some(...)` when chain is off, which would propagate into PrdRunResult.chain_base and visibly diverge — test must catch.
edgeCases: chain=true, later iteration: snapshot=Some(prev_branch); both sites read the same value.

failureModes.cause: Closure tries to move `chain_base_snapshot` instead of cloning
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Cargo check fails with E0507 — switch to `.clone()` inside the closure body.

userStories.notes: File: src/loop_engine/batch.rs. Re-read lines 633-707 first to confirm `chain_base` is not mutated between the closure definition (~653) and its two invocation sites (~668 and ~681). The reassignment at ~705 happens after both closure calls. Learning [2370]: deriving Default for result structs supports systematic refactor patterns — this task is a smaller-scope variant: hoist a repeated subexpression instead of restructuring the whole struct. Learning [528]: Option<String> + .clone() is the right pattern when borrowing isn't viable.
qualityDimensions: Single binding for the snapshot, used at both call sites (DRY)
Snapshot scope limited to the per-PRD iteration (not lifted to the outer scope)
No observable behavior change — verifiable by running existing batch.rs tests untouched
`grep` confirms exactly one `chain_base.clone()` remaining in lines 630-720 (the snapshot RHS)
userStories.title: Hoist chain_base snapshot out of make_result closure in batch.rs

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication: whitespace guard reuses the existing suppression pattern (eprintln! + early return), does NOT duplicate gate logic.

Functions under 30 lines after additions — if `maybe_fire` crosses 30 lines, flag for extraction (Learning [2402] notes guard-chains up to 71 lines are acceptable IF flat-return structured; use that as the upper bound).

Clear separation of concerns: `should_fire` remains pure-decision, `maybe_fire` remains orchestration, `prd_md_path` remains pure-lookup. New whitespace guard belongs in `maybe_fire` (launch-time boundary), NOT `should_fire` (decision-time).
Code follows the existing `[auto-review]` stderr prefix and recovery-hint convention.
Any issues found spawn `REFACTOR-FIX-xxx` tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`.
userStories.description:
Review the three FEAT-xxx changes for DRY, complexity, coupling, and pattern adherence.

DO: Check for duplicated logic (especially the new whitespace guard vs. existing gates in maybe_fire — does it follow the same shape?), functions > 30 lines after the additions, coupling between auto_review.rs gates, and naming consistency with the existing `[auto-review]` stderr prefix.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before final review. Do NOT introduce new abstractions for one-shot uses (the whitespace guard is a one-shot — do not extract a `validate_md_path` helper unless a second caller needs it).
userStories.notes: If no issues: emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line 'Clean refactor pass — guard placement, doc additions, and chain_base hoist all idiomatic.' If issues: spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`. Learning [2402]: guard-chain functions up to 71 lines acceptable if flat-return structured. Learning [2394]: maybe_fire is the shared gateway — keep additions consistent with the gateway shape.
qualityDimensions: DRY across the new whitespace guard vs. existing gates
Single-responsibility: should_fire / prd_md_path / maybe_fire boundaries preserved
Pattern consistency with existing `[auto-review]` stderr convention
No premature extraction for one-shot helpers
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: whitespace guard at maybe_fire's launch-time boundary is reachable from both src/main.rs::Commands::Loop and src/loop_engine/batch.rs::run_batch (grep-verified — both call `auto_review::maybe_fire`).
Positive: `cargo doc --no-deps 2>&1 | grep -i 'warning.*auto_review' | wc -l` returns `0` (rustdoc additions don't trip cargo doc warnings).
Positive: FULL `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` all clean (no scope filter).
Positive: `grep -n 'chain_base.clone()' src/loop_engine/batch.rs` shows the duplication collapsed (single occurrence in the snapshot RHS).

Negative: No `unwrap()` introduced in production code paths (tests OK). `grep -nE '\.unwrap\(\)' src/loop_engine/auto_review.rs src/loop_engine/batch.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]'` must show no new prod hits relative to main.
Negative: No dead code warnings for new code (the new whitespace guard, new test, and new doc comments are all reachable).
Negative: Auto-review failure does NOT change loop/batch exit code (re-verify by reading `maybe_fire` return value handling at the two call sites — `let _ = ...` or no binding).

Documentation: project CLAUDE.md's `## Auto-launch /review-loop after loop end` section already documents the whitespace footgun (added during /compound) — REVIEW-001 verifies that documentation matches the implemented guard's behavior (suppress, hint, no quote-attempt).

Task update: if any FEAT's implementation drifted from this PRD's acceptance criteria, update the JSON via `task-mgr init --from-json tasks/auto-review-followups.json --append --update-existing` (preserves status, refreshes descriptions). This is the only task that should do post-hoc edits.
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or spawned as FIX-xxx with verifyCommand if > ~12 unrelated).
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` (priority 50-97).
userStories.description:
Review all three FEAT changes for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Verify the whitespace guard is reachable from both call sites (main.rs single-loop AND batch.rs end-of-batch) — since both go through `maybe_fire`, the guard applies to both. Verify the rustdoc additions render correctly under `cargo doc`. Verify the chain_base hoist doesn't change observable behavior by running the full batch.rs test module. Run full `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check` with NO scope filter.
DO NOT: scope the test suite at this gate — full-suite green is the contract. Do NOT mark done if pre-existing failures regressed.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` — atomic DB+JSON sync. If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note. Cross-check: the project CLAUDE.md addition from /compound (worktree commit 918640a) names the whitespace footgun — verify the implemented FEAT-001 guard matches that documented behavior. Learning [2411]: maybe_fire return value unused at both call sites — this is the load-bearing invariant for the exit-code contract; do NOT break it.
qualityDimensions: No unwrap in production
All new code wired and reachable from production entry points
Full suite green including pre-existing
CLAUDE.md documentation matches implemented behavior
Exit-code invariant preserved (maybe_fire return value unused at call sites)
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
